rationale: Both correctly identify the same root cause (PR #4527 broke the non-async-chunk payload split to None, starving accumulate_full_payload_output) and the same resolution (already fixed by merged PR #4792, folded into the 0.24 rebase commit a560ed18), matching the thread's actual disposition to close with an upgrade/workaround path. X is more thoroughly grounded, citing many specific file:line locations across the scheduler coordinator, both model runners, and utils, and directly engages reporter-specific details (explains away the NVFP4 patch log line actually present in the issue body) with three concrete remediation options. Y is accurate and well-structured but cites fewer locations and doesn't engage the reporter's specific environment/log details as deeply.
